name: LinkedIn (official API)
summary: Publish posts, read your profile, and list feed activity over the official LinkedIn REST API, exposed as linkedin:// URI routes.
description: LinkedIn is an external urirun connector that talks to the official LinkedIn REST API (https://api.linkedin.com). It uses OAuth 2.0 access tokens with approved scopes (r_liteprofile, r_member_social, w_member_social) — never the login/password flow and never browser automation. Routes: publish a text/media post (UGC Posts API), read your own profile, list your recent posts. Credentials are addressed by reference (getv://LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN or secret://keyring/linkedin#token) and resolved through the urirun secrets layer (deny-by-default); an empty token falls back to LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN env var. Every write route is gated by urirun's --execute on the registry runner, so a binding without --execute cannot publish.
useCases: Publish a text post to LinkedIn from a urirun flow (official API, ToS-compliant).
Read your own profile fields (name, headline, member URN) for sanity-check before a flow.
List your recent posts to verify a publish landed.
requires: a LinkedIn Developer application with w_member_social + r_member_social + r_liteprofile scopes
an OAuth 2.0 access token (LINKEDIN_ACCESS_TOKEN) and your member URN (LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN)
